zh.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville是Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville的女兒，於大約1282年出生在英國奧可福爾勞頓。她於1305年1月與Guy Gobaud結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1334年2月28日在英國享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville是Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville的女儿，于大约1282年出生在英国奧可福爾勞頓。她于1305年1月与Guy Gobaud结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1334年2月28日在英国享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Coleville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Colleville in Seine-Maritime, France, named with the Scandinavian personal name Koli (see Cole 2) + Old French ville ‘settlement, village’. The Scottish branch of the family were descended from Philip de Colville, who came to Scotland as a household knight of kings Malcolm IV (1153–65) and William ‘the Lion’ (1165–1214).

variants.name: Cole
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colvin
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal, Antrim): variant of   Colville  , probably reflecting a local pronunciation. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th century.
Manx: if not identical with 1, perhaps from the Old Norse personal name Kolbeinn, with /v/ substituted for /b/.

English: from the rare Middle English personal name Colwin, Colvin, which may be a borrowing into English of a Welsh name whose modern form is Collwyn ‘white’, or of colwyn ‘doe, puppy, pet dog’, or of the placename Colwyn (Denbighshire, Radnorshire).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville was born about 1282, in Launton, Oxfordshire, England as the daughter of Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville. She married Guy Gobaud in January 1305. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 28 February 1334, in England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville est née environ 1282, à Launton, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, fille de Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville. Elle a épousé Guy Gobaud en janvier 1305. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 février 1334, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville wurde um 1282 in Launton, Oxfordshire, England, als Tochter von Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville geboren. Sie hat im Januar 1305 Guy Gobaud geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Februar 1334 im Alter von 53 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville è nata il circa 1282 nel Launton, Oxfordshire, England come figlia di Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville. Lei sposato Guy Gobaud nel gennaio 1305. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 28 febbraio 1334, nel England, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Colevilleは約1282年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアラウントンでRoger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colvilleの娘として生まれた。1305年1月にGuy Gobaudと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1334年2月28日にイギリスで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville(은)는 대략 1282년 영국 스퍼드실르 로턴에서 Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1305년 1월 Guy Gobaud(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1334년 2월 28일 영국에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville nasceu aproximadamente 1282, em Launton, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra filha de Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville. Ela casou-se com Guy Gobaud em janeiro de 1305. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1334, em Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice de Coleville родилась около 1282 г. в местности лаунтон, оксфордшир, Англия. Её отца звали Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy Gobaud январь 1305 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 28 февраля 1334 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1334, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
